heading: Disbursing officers’ claims
label: 1496 Disbursing officers’ claims
text: The United States Court of Federal Claims shall have jurisdiction to render judgment upon any claim by a disbursing officer of the United States or by his administrator or executor for relief from responsibility for loss, in line of duty, of Government funds, vouchers, records or other papers in his charge.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 250(3) (, ; , ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 14536 Stat. 1136June 10, 1921, ch. 18, § 30442 Stat. 24Words “paymaster, quartermaster, commissary of subsistence, or other,” preceding “disbursing officer of the United States,” were omitted. See , 1907, 42 Ct.Cl. 449 and , 1881, 17 Ct.Cl. 189, holding that the term “other disbursing officer” extends to any disbursing officer of the executive departments of the Government.Henderson v. United StatesHobbs v. United StatesWords “by capture or otherwise” were omitted as surplusage.Words “and for which such officer was and is held responsible,” at the end of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., were omitted as surplusage.section 250(3) of title 28Changes were made in phraseology.
Amendments1992— substituted “United States Court of Federal Claims” for “United States Claims Court”.Pub. L. 102–5721982— substituted “United States Claims Court” for “Court of Claims”.Pub. L. 97–164
Effective Date of 1992 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 102–572section 911 of Pub. L. 102–572section 171 of this titleOct. 29, 1992
